steps.assistant_text: Sibling-port test (per docs/MATCHING_WORKFLOW.md#feedback-port-matched-sibling-c-before-trusting-frame-regalloc-cap-claim): ported the matched titproc_uso_func_00002980 / mgrproc_uso_func_00003358 C body verbatim. The prior wrap had control-flow shape 'if (p != NULL) { init }' — switching to 'if (p == 0) return 0; init;' (early-return form, init unconditional) produces byte-identical match. Prior 60-89% NM cap claim about 'register allocation differs / frame layout' was misdiagnosis — the wrong C control-flow shape was the entire issue. Same lesson as 33358/2980 sibling-port.

metadata.verification: Byte-identical via objdump compare against expected/.o (36 insns, frame -0x28). All 0x90 bytes match.
